title: [On the Evolutionary, Bio-Psychological Foundations of the Human-Animal Relationship].

assets.abstract: Ever since, people live in contact with nature and animals, even in relatively non-utilitarian ways. Erich Fromm and Edward Wilson termed this human universal "Biophilia". But why different species can live together in a social way, is explained by a "common social toolbox" of neural, psychological and physiologicalmechanisms, which evolved over phylogeny.Major components of this toolbox are found in the vertebrate brain, which evolved over the past 600 million years in a succession of key innov

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.
